reviews.review_description:
THE GOOD:
•	Voice acting and delivery is quality and the dialogue / lines are fine
•	I’ve played a 100+ VR games but nothing like this before, It’s a fairly unique / cool concept

THE BAD:
•	The character models aren’t motion captured. The limbs and hands are contorted and their animations while walking and moving around are janky, it’s uncanny valley stuff. This ruins immersion.
•	None of the characters are likeable and there’s not enough character development to really care about any of them.
•	There is nothing intriguing / interesting about the story (so far – see my last BAD point).
•	Only two locations* (apartment and office) plus MR mode, so visually it’s fairly limited
•	They refer to Mixed Reality (MR) mode, but it’s actually just AR/passthrough because it's not anchored to or interacting with your real environment!. The board physically moves to a random spot in my IRL room everytime I switch between VR and “MR” mode. Plus, the clues on the detective board also move everytime too, and just becomes a randomised spaghetti mess!
•	There is no hint system (it definitely needs one as the last clue(s) in a scene can be difficult and frustrating to find)
•	The game is fundamentally boring because there isn’t any detective-like cognitive deduction required. The game loop consists of finding clues in a VR scene by photographing them, then switch to viewing all clues on the MR detective board. Take each clue in turn and  a voice over will explain it's significance in context to the story. Then search the clue for (a) blood, (b) fingerprints and a (c) person link [e.g. name on a letter]; then pull the string from the clue to one these three icons. It then enables you to view their testimony about that clue. Basically, drip feeding you a part of the story (rather than the all-important, ‘being a detective’).
•	I encountered a bug stopper 3/4 of the way through and couldn’t progress the game past scene 8 to Laura.

I love this app, you get to play Crime Scene Investigator & Detective! The graphics are great and it is intriguing. This app is very engaging, I thought I would do half an hour session on it but ended up spending an hour and a half! Still lots more crime scenes and clues to work through. Thank you to the developers, I hope you do more. 🕵️‍♀️🫆🩸🆎🖋️📑

Honestly, this is a two star game, but I gave it an extra star because the team is small and I have to give them credit for what they did here. However, there are some serious issues. 
But, let's start with the positives. 
First, I enjoyed it! It is a quick experience that was fun! It kept me engaged and had a generally good story (note on that at the end).
Next, the voice acting is very good. I felt like they were actually acting out the scenes and not just running lines.
Third, I liked the mixed reality portion where you come back from the scene and can move around in the real world with your corkboard. It was a nice addition that was unnecessary, but completely welcome. 
Now the negative.
One that hit me right away is that if you get stuck, not sure where the clue is and what to take a picture of, that's it! I'm not saying the game is difficult, but if you're not sure what to do, that's it and the game is over; you cannot proceed. It really needs a hint or clue system. 
Second, the character models are....bad. They are choppy, float rather than walk, and are stiff. They just look weird and janky. Their arthritic hands are painful to look at and they clip through both themselves and objects they are meant to interact with. If they looked like their photo likeness, we would be golden. But sadly they don't.
And finally, the story. I will not spoil anything (except maybe the general vibe, but if you really want to play this, maybe stop reading), but the story is good and intriguing, but concludes quite anticlimactically. I am an author and I know writing a story for a game is difficult, but the ending felt weak. The post game monologue by the narrator softened the blow and helped me understand the intent, but still, it was kind of like saying, "Never mind," to the whole experience you just had!
All this said, I do hope the studio makes another of these and adds some polish. Character models like 7th Guest (I know-major studio!) would have added a star easily!

I wanna make it clear that I understand that this is a small indie studio, but I have played many vr games made by small studios amd they do INFINITELY better than this piece of garbage. 

It is a painfully awful experience. I don't understand how this game is 30 dollars in the absolute slightest. This is worth at LEAST 10 dollars. 

First, the gameplay. Its repetitive and boring. You grab your magnifying glass and click a feature on it, find the clue, click the next feature, find the clue, and when you finish looking at it, then you connect the very obvious facts to the suspects and then you use these connections to "question" them. Some lead to absolutely nothing, and some lead to something that unlocks the next scene. 

The positives is that its quite novel for the first two times, and then it quickly AND I MEAN VERY VERY QUICKLY. Another positive is that the time traveling gimmick is quite cool. Seeing how each scene unfolds is quite fun. It never evolves anything or do anything interesting. Literally most of the time you read papers. I don't mind seeing the build up to the "murder" which we will get into, but the amount of buildup is so unbelievably long that the reveal of what happened feels like a slap to the face. We will get to that. 

The mixed reality which IS A BIG SELLING POINT of the game is practically useless and uncreative. It doesn't do anything interesting or advanced with the mixed reality setting at all. You literally stand in your area with a board filled with photos, and a investigation machine. I have seen cheaper games do this infinitely more better and creatively. 

**SPOILER** 
What about the mystery itself? Oh it was fun...until it wasn't. The massive most brilliant reveal of this garbage game was that Richard Hue, the supposed victim of this game that we spent around 3 or 4 hours trying to piece together on what happened and how did he die? What happened to him? Who did it? Well guess what?! He didn't die! He wasn't killed! And he instead, and no joke, decided to run away home to his mom to escape all of the stress and stuff effecting him.

This is not only a complete slap to the face, but the game digs the knife deeper by framing Richard as a spoiled jerk when in reality, almost everyone around him was even more awful than him. His ex girlfriend? Took advantage of his artistic skills and ultimately dumped him because her career was put first and called him a selfish jerk. The person interviewing him? Didn't even take his job seriously because he wasn't interesting in Richard and wanted to focus on other projects despite Richard wanting to take his profession seriously. The rich man that gave him a contract? Gave him an impossible deadline to release 3 collections or else he will owe him massive amounts of money. Even the ONE person that actually gave some care towards him was the one that set up the conflict of the story by giving him said contract. 

What sucks is that the story itself is pretty sad. Richard Hue was a massive artist who wanted to propell himself further in fame but due to all the stress and overall people surrounding him, he grew mentally unstable. But, no instead they just wanted to tell a pointless long story about how "people have motives and they sometimes put those motives first than people."

Speaking of the characters, the models are awful. They are janky, horribly animated, and incredibly janky. There is a scene where ex-gf and Richard was dancing and they weren't even holding hands at all despite the clear intention to, and their models just rubbed off of one another. There is another scene where he is supposed to be holding a paintbrush, but HE ISN'T. 

The voice acting? Almost all of it is awful. Everything from the mic quality to the audio balancing is borderline hard to hear the characters even on max volume. The only person that gave a decent performance is the one playing Karl Von Rove. His posh accent and overall dismissive nature was well communicated. Everyone else gave bland, horrible, unemotional preformances which makes sense cause almost every character is exactly that, bland. 

And finally, this game is lazy. The laziest game ive ever seen ever. Multiple dialogs were reused. I don't mind it when the characters say something like "how is this related to me?" Or "Yes thats my things what about it?" But there was times where the dialog was cut WAY TOO SOON, one time where the woman read her dismissive line, and then paused for 5 seconds, and said the second dismissive line and times where they physically take lines out of the "confessions" segments and reuse them during questioning them. This is lazy. Plain and simple. Some of the clues in this game borderline on pointless filler. There is a painting in Karl Von Rove's office of himself and THERE IS NO NARRATIVE OR STORY REASON OTHER THAN "he's egotistical" as if we haven't heard ANYTHING like that from the other characters. Another useless evidence was a postcard to Richard's mom which all is used for is to justify the existence of a pointless ending. 

This game is 30 dollars and being so positively reviewed is an insult to other indie devs who actually put effort and time into these games. This game is worthless and time wasting. I have never felt so unbelievably annoyed and bored while playing a vr game. It was a thoroughly worthless experience.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for taking the time to share such detailed feedback.
While the experience clearly didn’t resonate with you, I do appreciate that you played the game through to the end ! To be honest, as the author of the game, reading your words was quite painful. We pour our hearts and countless hours into these projects, so hearing terms like "lazy" or "worthless" is genuinely hurtful.

  Info : our  game is at $18 not $30.

Our intention was to offer a slower, narrative-driven investigation focused on ambiguity and human fragility rather than a conventional murder mystery. 
 It is very difficult to design a 5-6hours game designed around handtracking.


We understand this approach won’t work for everyone, and we’ll continue learning and improving with each project.  

Dear @BuddyAdams
As an indie studio, reviews and ratings have a direct impact on our visibility and our ability to keep building games, so we truly value thoughtful criticism. 

Best, 
Alexandre - Gamedesigner & writer of Detective VR

reviews.review_description: I absolutely loved this game. I do agree with the previous review but it is so rare that we get these types of games so I want to encourage this and any other teams looking to create similar games to keep going. Please add new DLCs for this game!! I would love to solve more cases!!! Thank you for all your work, time, and effort!!!!

Loved playing this game it’s very well made and the story was good too. Wish it lasted longer but was still worth it. Will there be other cases uploaded in the future? 

Only thing I can fault is it’s a little buggy at times and had to leave the game and re enter sometimes cos the board went to the ceiling. And you can’t resize the board so it takes up a lot of space on the screen and have to move it out the way to see the interviews but u get use to that, I’d have rather been able to resize the board thought. 

Hopping for more cases in the near future.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your feedback! 
I'm glad you enjoyed the investigation. 
Great suggestion: I'll add the option to change the size of the detective board in the next update!


We're a very small team of four. I wrote the game and did the game design all by myself. If the game does well, I'll tell other stories.

Feel free to change your rating to support us (even though four stars is already great)! 
Thank you very much.
You can join us on our Discord channel : https://discord.gg/yDJjPjuTjb

Have a great day! 
Alexandre

reviews.review_description:
Amazing work for a small studio. This game thrusts you into the narrative as a detective trying to solve a murder. Your very own who-done-it with a minority report twist, this game definitely satisfies my mystery vibes.

The mixed reality integration is also amazing. While the board and pedestal does not reset perfectly every time, the functionality of moving these objects around your mapped out space is very easy. 


Hand tracking is about 90% responsive and accurate, and also a great way to immerse oneself in the crime.

All and all amazing game and I look forward to updates and other installments!!!

developer_response.body:
Wow, thank you so much for this amazing review 🙏
I'm thrilled you enjoyed the narrative twist and the mixed reality integration : making players feel like the detective in their own space was exactly our goal! 

Hand tracking is still something we’re improving step by step, and your feedback motivates us to push it even further.

We’ll keep working hard to improve Detective VR! And if the game finds its audience, we’d love to dream about making a sequel one day 🔍🕵️


You can join our discord here to chat with us  : https://discord.gg/yDJjPjuTjb 

Thanks from all our indie team at Studio Chipo y Juan
Alex - gamedesigner of  Detective VR

reviews.review_title: Don't know how to pass to anything
reviews.review_description:
Bored. Got a board, evidence, nothing else is clear to me. How to unpin, doesn't say.
Why don't games give clear directions?
If stuck, what do we do?

developer_response.body:
Hi AbsterA,
Sorry to read that you are stuck ! 

You can delete a link by pinching the X in the middle of the line you just created.
You can move polaroid around 

Your goal : 
- Bring the polaroid to pedestal , scan every clue 
- Connect them to the suspect 
- Watch the VHS tape (by bringing them to the pedestal) 
- Check your goals on the objectif board 
- Then the suspect will unlock a new sequence for you !

You can look at this walkthrough video  to help you
https://youtu.be/Ybcsnw0kvQ0?si=OVISN93iW6y9zoog


Our game is made by an indie team of just 4 people :
 I did write and designed the gameplay
1 star is very though for us, it is very difficult to be visible , if you feel like it, feel free to change the rating ! Thank you 🙏 it will help us a lot !
 

You can reach me at alexandre@detective-vr.com 
Or join our discord https://discord.gg/yDJjPjuTjb to chat with the team

Thank you 
All the Best, 
Alex and the team of Detective VR

reviews.review_description: I wish I could give this game 10 stars!! The graphics are truly beautiful and life like,  the music lends to the vibe perfectly, and the voice overs of the instructions,  etc, are on point, clear, and concise.  It flawlessly leads you through a tutorial.... yet the tutorial itself is part of the mystery.  I experienced zero issues with the execution of moves,  as the controls are tight and work great. The storyline is engaging,  and I just can't wait to get back in headset tomorrow so I can play for a longer time and delve deeper into the mystery.  It's easy to play but challenging enough to keep you coming back for more.  I can NOT believe that this game was created with a team of just 4 fabulous people. If you're a fan of Shattered, but this game immediately  if not sooner!!!

developer_response.body:
Thank you a thousand times for this incredible review! It truly means the world to our indie team :  this is exactly why we make games!

We’re so happy you enjoyed it so much, and thrilled that our storytelling and gameplay resonated with you.

Thanks again ! 
We can’t wait to share news of our next game with you!
Alex, Quentin & Xavier

reviews.review_description: There are many things to commend about this game, particularly its creative use of MR. My only issue is that (no spoilers) after playing through the entire story, I found the ending to be unsatisfying. Even still, it’s lots of fun, and an interesting and original approach. I hope the developers will continue to create new titles .

developer_response.body:
Thank you for finishing the game ! 
I know the ending was a bit of a gamble : it’s surprising and intentionally unsettling, going against expectations. But since the very first mention of Richard Hue in the intro, there are clues pointing in that direction. I tried to include subtle hints at every stage, and most of the evidence actually gives you the full picture right from the start.

I’m sorry if the ending disappointed you!

Our game is made by an indie team of just 4 people,
 I did write and designed the gameplay
⭐️If you feel like it, don’t hesitate to put 5 stars to support us 💫 
 it helps so much with the visibility of the game on the store! 
Under 4,7 rating we will not be featured in the Mixed-Reality / narrative page.
 Every sale of our game is re-invested in time to update and improve it

Thank you for your support, 
All the Best, 
Alex and the team of Detective VR

reviews.review_description: Love the game. Exploring the room in different sections after completing parts. It was a bit buggy. The characters jumped into different areas playing video tapes. Loved looking for fingerprints ect  figuring how who matches which puzzle. I've not completed it yet but really enjoying it. :) I recommend if you enjoy becoming a detetective in vr. I like the rewind time function .

reviews.review_description: Essentially a mixed reality visual novel, there is no fail state, you just keep investigating until you find the clues you need to progress. I’m a little less than halfway through and really enjoying it. I love the time rewind mechanic, it reminds me of the detective mode sequences in Arkham VR (the one from 2017), which was criminally underrated. I have to admit, I got stuck for a little bit until I realized I could return to earlier scenes to finish examining clues and open up new scenes.
Game needs A LOT of work

reviews.review_title: Impressive effort for a small team
reviews.review_description:
Detective VR makes a strong first impression. The big, clear buttons and simple mechanics make it easy to step into the role of investigator, and at first it’s satisfying to build a detective wall, connect evidence, and use the magnifying glass to uncover details.

The novelty doesn’t last. Once you’ve learned the flow - take photo, connect string, drop VHS tape, watch a witness clip - the process becomes repetitive. Many clues feel obvious or redundant, and dialogue lines repeat until they’re more annoying than atmospheric. The evidence board also has quirks: in my small living room it often reappeared embedded in walls or furniture and would not stay where placed. The VHS icons piled up on top of each other making them difficult to grasp. Hand tracking is offered but drifts too easily, so controllers are the safer choice.

On the plus side, Detective VR is comfortable to play, the music is pleasant, and the British voices add some atmosphere. But by the end, the mystery dragged on and the resolution felt anticlimactic. There are some fun moments early on, but overall it plays more like cataloging chores than solving a gripping case.

As someone still new to VR, while I didn’t always enjoy the repetition, I respect the ambition of this four-person team. I’m glad I got to experience this game.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much Janine  for taking the time to share such a  honest and detailed review 🙏

I understand your concerns about repetition and hand tracking :
You’re absolutely right about the repetition : I thought it will help the players  to focus & dive into the story, and in the end a new chronological  mechanic appears to refresh the investigation. 

These are fair points, and we’re already thinking about how to improve these aspects for future updates. The goal of Detective VR was to experiment with narrative investigation in mixed reality, and it means a lot that you recognized our effort.. 

For the pile of VHS you can reposition them on your board and also delete the wrong one.



 For the repeated lines, we already planned an update.
We’re a tiny indie team, and your feedback really helps us improve.

I know no game is perfect :
 ⭐️If you feel like it, don’t hesitate to put 5 stars to support us 💫 – it helps so much with the visibility of the game on the store! 
Under 4,7 rating we will not be featured in the Mixed-Reality / narrative page. Every sale of our game is re-invested in time to update and improve it

UPDATE FOR NOVEMBER : 
- Adding new line
- New Gameplay feature to validate a line on your detective board
- More tutorial (Watch  / Gameplay ) 
- adding a Interrogation point button 
 - Change teleportation hands (left / right)
- free locomotion
- Recap at the beginning of a playthrough
- slider opacity dark mask in MR when a suspect appears
- slider music / voice in VR
-solve glitch distance grab  
- improvement in Handtracking accuracy

You’re very welcome to join our Discord and share more feedback: https://discord.gg/yDJjPjuTjb

Thanks from all our indie team
Alex - Gamedesigner of Detective VR

reviews.review_description:
I'm really enjoying Detective VR so far, especially since it hasn't triggered my migraines or motion sickness. The only time I've felt a little off is when I get so absorbed in the game that I forget to take a break.
​I've already completed about three or four crime boards. The few glitches I've run into have been easy to fix just by going back and forth between the crime board and the crime scene. I love having the crime board and the clue investigation pedestal to work with—it's such a nice touch, especially being able to further investigate the clues. As an '80s baby, the Polaroids and VHS tapes really make me happy! The game is strangely addictive; I have to force myself to take a break but always want to get back to it as soon as possible.
​The timeline feature is great. The way it lets you pause it yourself but also stops automatically makes the game feel more relaxed and less rushed. I also really like how the timelines are acted out instead of just being a series of crime scenes. It feels like there's a great balance between the two.

​Whilst I'm having fun, there are a few quality-of-life changes that would make the game even better:
​- Make it easier to adjust from standing to sitting. A simple toggle option would be a huge help for people with limited mobility or chronic illnesses.
​-Improve the controls. Some things, like moving the clues sheet, work best with hand controls, while others, like teleporting, are better with the controllers. Having more reliability across both options would be great.
​-Some actions could be more intuitive. It's easy to accidentally grab a pin instead of a Polaroid, so a little less fiddling around would be nice.
​-A brief case intro. It would be great to have a short summary at the start of the case, something like, "A call came in about a suspected murder at X location..." This would help set the scene rather than just dropping you in. Also, more cases! 😅
​-More movement options. Having a locomotion option in addition to teleporting would be a nice touch.
​-Better sound options. Sometimes the voices are too quiet or get drowned out by the background music, so more sound customisation would be helpful.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your review  and all your feedback , I totally agree : 
 We're working on a big update ! 


Keep in mind, that we are an indie team of 4 : we'll do our best !
Update (for November I think) : 
-Sitting / standing setting
- Change teleportation hands (left / right)
- free locomotion

- slider music / voice in VR
-solve glitch distance grab in MR
- Working on a better intro

About a second part of the story, we'd love to : 
Finger crossed, if enough people play our game will be able to do a sequel ! 


⭐️If you feel like it, don't hesitate to put 5 stars to support us 💫 it'll help so much the visibility of our game  ! Thank you for your support 🙌

You can join our discord here and chat with us : https://discord.gg/yDJjPjuTjb 

All the best,
Thanks from all our indie team at Studio Chipo y Juan
Alex - Gamedesigner of Detective VR

reviews.review_title: An interesting and unique "who did it"
reviews.review_description:
Teleport around scenes (I generally prefer free locomotion, but teleport works for this game). Each set piece is small enough (like an apartment room) that free locomotion simply isn't needed. 

It's more like reading a mystery novel out of order as each scene brings you to different events that occurred that you need to collect evidence from. 

It's impossible to fail (which brings me back to my mystery novel analogy - you finish the book or you don't); however my one gripe would be at the end you need to put the events in order and I found this to be extremely ambiguous. 

The hand tracking is 98% good and I would say the better way to play. 

It is a very small studio, so don't go in expecting AAA character models, but the sets look good and I would say it was decent voice acting. I feel they did a great job at delivering an experience.

reviews.review_title: Cool Mixed Reality Approach to Detective
reviews.review_description:
I loved piecing together clues and solving puzzles.
I was super excited about this detective game.
You are the hero, gathering evidence and interrogating suspects in a virtual world..
You will solve murders and uncover conspiracies.
I think the game gets the mechanics of exploring and analyzing clues, right, but I wished free roaming around instead of point and click system. But I understand why they picked this style to have more control of the angles of the cameras for the atmosphere. 
I would have liked more freedom. 
Hand tracking works for the most part. However, there are still some glitches trying to start the videotapes.
Also, I wish there was a more visual quick tutorial on how the mechanics work.
Room for improvement but a good start.

developer_response.body:
Thank you very much Rucacha for this incredible 5 stars review and your feedbacks on the game mechanics !

reviews.review_description:
Detective VR made me feel like a real investigator straight out of a thriller. The mix of MR, time-control mechanics, and hand tracking is brilliant, it really puts you in the middle of the case. I first tried it sitting at the office… big mistake. Let’s just say some desks didn’t survive my "investigative spirit". Next time I’ll play in the living room! 
A fun, smart, and truly immersive experience. Highly recommended!

You collect and analyze clues forming connections to various suspects. The game play is smooth, voice acting superb, and graphics mind blowing. I thoroughly enjoyed my experience and highly recommend this game!
I loved the concept of switching between virtual crime scenes and a mixed reality investigation board! The hand tracking is excellent! This is the first game I've played entirely without controllers!

developer_response.body:
Thanks a lot for your review 🙌
We’re so happy you’re enjoying the investigation and  hand-tracking control (it was not easy to make it natural) ! 🔍 


You can join our discord here : https://discord.gg/yDJjPjuTjb 
Thanks from all our indie team at Studio Chipo y Juan
Alex - Gamedesigner of Detective VR

reviews.review_description: As others have said, the ending kind of pulls the rug out. It is also buggy in ways that could be easily fixed. And the animation is so-so. But, I give it 4 stars because the concept is unique enough to make it recommended and it was done by a small studio.

reviews.review_title: 🔍 Step Into the Mind of a Detective
reviews.review_description:
This game offers one of the most immersive investigative experiences I've had in VR. You truly feel like a crime scene investigator — taking photos, linking clues, and slowly unraveling the truth. What makes it stand out is the brilliant transition between VR and MR, adding a new level of realism as you switch environments to find new evidence or connect details others might miss.

Every scene feels like you're part of a gripping thriller — you’re at the center of the mystery, and the pressure to observe everything is real. It’s clever, intense, and incredibly well executed. The stress of missing a clue is balanced by the satisfaction of solving each piece of the puzzle.

If you enjoy detective stories, escape rooms, or immersive puzzle games, this is a must-have. A very polished and unique experience — it absolutely deserves a place in any quality VR library.
Took me a wee bit to figure out and since then have been having a blast.  Not too hard that you can’t figure out, but not too easy where you sail through the game.  Hope part 2 is in the works!

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your review !  We're working on a big update ! 
About a second part of the story, we'd love to : 
Finger crossed, if enough people play our game will be able to do a sequel !

 You can join our discord here : https://discord.gg/yDJjPjuTjb 
Ps- what was the part of gameplay you did figure out at the beginning : let us now , we'll try to improve it

Alex - Gamedesigner of Detective VR

reviews.review_description:
What an amazing game!
I truly felt like a real detective. Looking for clues, and taking photos of the evidence was my favorite part!
Highly recommend if you like detective games!
It is a must!! ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️
This game is pretty fun. Even if it is repetitive.

⭐️Don't hesitate to put 5 stars to support us 💫 it'll help so much the visibility of our game  ! Thank you for your support 🙌

Thanks from all our tiny indie team at Studio Chipo y Juan
Alex - Writer & Gamedesigner of Detective VR

reviews.review_description: This is a great game! It was a lot of fun. It is an easy play that also had some challenging parts, all of which made it a great experience. Not too tough where you want to give up and not too easy where you get bored. It was a good balance. I hope there will be more cases in the future to continue the adventure. When I came across a bug that halted my progress, the development team quickly responded and resolved the issue. I highly recommend!

I was pleasantly surprised by how immersive this game was, I used the hand tracking as it felt more natural and worked really well, looking for clues is one thing, but then working with the board and trying to piece together clues is something else!
Really enjoying it so far, will update once I've finished it.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for this great review ! 
Your comment made my day. 

For our indie team of 4, every comment counts ,thanks !
I hope you'll enjoy the end of the investigation !
Alex , gamedesigner of Detective VR

reviews.review_description: Wish there was more to the game. There’s only one story and it only takes 2-3 hours. Thank you for such a quick response and offering so much! I never meant to imply the game wasnt great just wish there was more to play!

developer_response.body:
Hello Imagine_Wagons69.2025, 
Thank you for playing our game: 
indeed there is only one story spanning 13 sequences (between 2.5 and 5 hours of gameplay). 
I'm delighted that you were drawn into the story; you must have raced through the sequences! 

Did you enjoy the story and the characters? 
You can discuss it with our team on Discord https://discord.gg/yDJjPjuTjb

We are a small team of four people, and we did our best to tell a compelling story within our means. 
We would love to tell more stories in the future, as we have lots of ideas. The best way to support us is to leave a good review to promote the game. 

Thank you ! 
All the best,
Alex, the gamedesigner and writer of Detective VR

reviews.review_description: The environments are amazing and the mixed reality/hand tracking makes it extremely immersive. Holding and interacting with objects is is best I’ve used. Can’t wait to play more!

If you enjoy story-driven mysteries rather than action/horror, If you like puzzling through clues, interrogating unreliable witnesses, exploring environments carefully, this will hit the mark, and definitely the game for you. This is an excellent mixed reality game. I shall look forward to the next whodunit

developer_response.body:
Thank yo use much for your excellent review, I'm so happy you enjoyed our mixed-reality investigation ! 

Your review made my day ! For an indie team like us, it's what give us motivation to continue ! 

 All the best, 
Alex- Gamedesigner of Detective VR

reviews.review_description: I enjoyed the game so far. The game plays out like a storybook and I was disappointed with the fact that I didn’t have any narrative choice. You go around the room scanning for clues and taking Polaroids. You then connect the clues back to fingerprints or blood types.  For me the parts that sent you back mixed reality also took me out of the game because I see the mess left behind by my children last night and I just wanted to pout the game down and give them a price of my mind. The graphics remind me of playing on Xbox 360, but that has never been a major concern for me. Initially it wasn’t clear what I was supposed to be doing and I wasted an ample amount of time. Through trial and error I got the hang of it and the story progressed faster. I didn’t realize each link to the Polaroids came with a vhs tape for you to watch. The game does seem to be a lot of watching and little action. Actually with all the blood apogee I am not sure who died Agatha or Richard, but that could because I was lazily clicking things without engaging in the story. I actually think it would make sense to have a case file to read before you start to get an overview of the crime before you were thrust onto the scene. Or even an officer voiceover before it begins giving some exposition.

developer_response.body:
Thank you very much SoloZilMOrd  for your review and your feedbacks on the game mechanics : I agree with you about the lack of direction (I will follow you advice and add very soon a "Case reminder" and additional tutorial)

About the 360 graphics, we tried our best but it's a game running on Quest 2 & 3 using Mixed-Reality and Handpicking (that use also a lot of processing power )! I hope you enjoyed the variety of the scene and the lightning .

We're working on a big update we'll do our best (keep in mind that we are an indie team of 4 people putting all our effort in Detective VR) : 
- More tutorial (Watch  / Gameplay ) 
- adding a"Interrogation point button" that trigger tutorial 
- "Case reminder " features
 - Change teleportation hands (left / right)
- free locomotion
- Recap at the beginning of a playthrough
- slider opacity dark mask in MR when a suspect appears
- slider music / voice in VR
- solve glitch distance grab  
- improvement in Hand-Tracking accuracy



🙏If you feel like it, don't hesitate to put 5 stars to support us (it'll help so much the visibility ! ✨ Thank you 🙌 ! 
You can join our discord here : https://discord.gg/yDJjPjuTjb 

Thanks from all our indie team
Alex - Gamedesigner of Detective VR

reviews.review_title: The Crown Jewel of VR experiences!
reviews.review_description:
This might be the first review I’ve ever written for a VR product, but this is also the only VR experience I have been able to play for more than 40 mins at a time so it’s 100% well-deserved and here’s why 🕵️‍♂️

Story: Agatha Christie level of well-written characters, Danganronpa like twists and Ace Attorney like mechanics, Detective VR is a triumph of narrative and murder mystery experiences. Everything comes cohesively together to create an engaging narrative, where not only will you struggle to figure out whodunnit, you’ll get to literally see the characters, their memories and their lies in front of you and have the chance to bring all their skeletons out of the closet. The A-Class voice acting a cut above most games and stellar soundtrack will also immerse you completely, this is the first experience I actually moved around objects in game rather then through them!  

Mechanics: this game is not a walk in the park even for the seasoned murder mystery aficionado, you’ll be using the amazing magnifying glass feature to look under every table and behind every wall for fingerprints and blood because the game is not going to make it easy for you, especially if you want to unlock all  concealed truths characters are hiding from you.

Ultimately, if you are looking for the VR experience to not only debate culprits at the dinner table with your friends and family but also to recreate that childhood feeling of picking up a book like Harry Potter and getting completely lost in the characters, stories and magic of another world, this is a must play experience. Not doing so would be a real crime against yourself  :0

reviews.review_title: Had bugs but liked the concept
reviews.review_description:
Update:  kudos to the developer for a lightening fast response and a message, this bug has been fixed and I have finished the game! I like the concept and enjoyed playing, my biggest complaint is the price and length, If more content was added such as other cases, it would be more worth the price and putting in chronological order was not fun.


Let me first start by saying I have been playing VR since the first Quest came out and I'm also a pcvr player. I have come across glitches and bugs but none that stops me in my tracks from playing a game any further. Furthermore if this isn't fixed I guess you're out of money because you get to this point right about 2 and 1/2 hours in to the game. I think many of the reviews were from people that haven't gotten to this point. When you play Karl's confession tape, all of a sudden you have audio playing on top of itself, you see a black screen with an outline of a room that moves when you move your headset. It just freezes and you can't go any further. I am frustrated and the developers need to fix this right away I cannot recommend this game at all to anyone until this is fixed. I will then rate it accordingly. I have rebooted and restarted my headset and uninstalled and reinstalled about five times... nothing works.

I really was looking forward to this! The idea is cool for sure, prefer the MR stuff over when it puts you into VR as it was a little dangerous then teleporting around the VR space but then turning and slightly moving in my real space so was completely losing where i was trying to stay stood.

The game seems to just do the work for you rather then you actualy solve things, grab a photo, aim the magnifying glass and click through the 3 tools, no skill to it just gauranteed result everytime.

Then for the hour or so i played i seem to just be putting videos on and watchin parts of a conversation which i had no idea the context but apparently i did the right things as i got green ticks and it went onto the next scene / location.. i was kinda like "ahh ok, i guess i did something there"

Im not sure if its a few cases or it seemed to feel like it was going to be 1 long 1 but i may be wrong (hopefully its not just the one)

The one thing that defo needs updating is the location you place the board.

I moved it to 1 of my real walls and then moved the pedestal to a position that would have the projection of the person in the centre of my room, but upon returning to the board after each area it would just then randomly have the board somewhere else in my room and id keep having to place it back on my wall.

Got a little annoying after the 4th time, it defo needs to remember the location you leave it, especially if its taking in the room setup of the scan etc.

Maybe ill come back to this another time but for now unfortunately i felt pretty underwhelmed and ill most likely be refunding it.

Love how engaged the game makes you feel, having to take notice of bits of small detail watching the crime scene unfold and having to look for the clues, the last part the chronological order is of the stressful side but that's where you have to use your actual brain and identify what's gone on, 10/10 for me really enjoyed this game would like to see a number 2 or DLC added

Detective VR really is delightful. It’s rare that such a high quality narrative (story-driven) game comes along. Both VR newbies and veterans will find this engaging: you don’t need those cumbersome controllers because it uses intuitive hand gestures instead. Very immersive!  

It puts you in the shoes of a detective, encouraging you to physically nose around as you hunt or clues , some of which are only visible at certain moments or from certain angles. As more scenes and characters are introduced, and more clues are analysed, you gain a fresh perspective, delivering satisfying rushes of insight. The clever story driver makes you WANT to know exactly what did happen, and in what sequence (like in that Christopher Nolan movie, ‘Memento’).

Each time you piece together a set of clues, you are given a new crime scene with a new part of the storyline or a new character to examine. This feels like a huge reward. The scrubbing through storylines – where the NPCs replay scenes from the past – is a wonderful interaction allowing you to gain satisfying new insight as the characters’ true motivations are gradually revealed.

The switching between MR and VR is also an inspired touch, both practically – allowing respite from VR fatigue which allowed me to stay in headset much longer than usual – but also, in story terms: by returning to the evidence board in MR (back at the police station) from VR (each crime scene), it provided a safe and familiar space in which I could take my time interpreting the collected evidence. 

This game is so much fun. It’ll keep you guessing and playing for hours at a time!

reviews.review_title: Game is not game, use brain
reviews.review_description:
Very great the story. I want be a detective when I grow up. This gave me a few hints what I should to do in my future. The tools are limited but in real life, you can have multi tools. 

Thank you for making this “game” actually it was little annoying to me when use hand tracking and some glitches. 

Hand tracking: left hand is weak than right hand. Left hand is freezing when I use. Hand menu should be remove on face palm until you click finger to open menu during play. 

Glitches: sometimes the bar where you will move  poster will disappear. You have to restart to see if the bar appear to move it. The tablet where you have to solve it or watch short video, would soft lock, where you can’t put it to open until less 3 minutes or restart it. There are a few glitches but can’t put it more in this reviews. 

Characters: looked very funny the texture body and the hand. Like shake hand with hold the camera. 


Thank you!!! My dream work is I’m detective!

reviews.review_title: I heard the developers are nice people
reviews.review_description: Am I a bad person for rating my own game ?

The best VR Detective game I’ve ever played. I love how you only use your hands. Very addictive game, very well designed, great actors portraying the numerous characters, so much detail in this game. Made me feel like a real detective. Highly recommend. Much respect to the team behind this game, great job!

This was a really good game. I would’ve given it. Five stars had it not been so glitchy so often. I really like the way that clues are collected and suspects are investigated. I look forward to more like this, a little more polished.

This is one experience not to miss out on.
The tutorial takes you through the basics premise and its very clear what needs to be done.
The way the vr transitions to mr is astounding and works so well, a big space is helpful but everything can be moved to fit your space.

You are thrown in to the murder scene and straight away you really feel like a detective when the crime board appears and you have to piece everything together together.
The story is engaging, I’m fully involved and you can feel the tension between the characters.

A definite winner that you must own.

reviews.review_title: A new kind of puzzle game is born
reviews.review_description:
I wasn't sure that I will be amazed by Detective VR. But after the first hour, I understood that it was a new kind of VR game that I havn't seen until now in VR.
The game is not perfect but very accessible with a story that held me till the end.
I like the way we have to analyze and follow a scene. So as the second phase when we have to examine all evidence and ask suspects about the fact.
With MR and Hand Tracking, I think this is a perfect game to start VR and to show it to our friend and family.
Not the best game but one of the game that impressed me in many points.
Congratulations to the studio.

reviews.review_title: I was living this app and then
reviews.review_description: Suddenly videotapes won’t lower and I can’t listen to them. There was an update announced just yesterday which I thought would fix it- it didn’t

developer_response.body:
Hi Gladbell,
UPDATE : Problem video tape FIXED  08/05/25 version 134 

I’m really sorry you ran into a bug with the VHS tapes : I’m here to help, and we’ll get this sorted out together!
I just double-checked and everything seems to be working fine on the latest version. Could you try fully quitting the app and check if it’s updated to the most recent version?

To help us understand what’s going wrong, few questions :
- Are you playing on Meta Quest 2 or Quest 3?
- Are you using your left or right hand to interact?
- Where are you currently in the story? which sequence ? The VHS bug is in all sequences ? 
If you can, feel free to send us a short gameplay video at contact@detective-vr.com, that would be super helpful. Also, don’t hesitate to join our Discord to chat directly with the team https://discord.gg/yDJjPjuTjb !

Again sorry for the bug, I'm on it : I'll fix it !
We're here to make sure you have a great time with the investigation.
Alex,Game Designer of Detective VR

reviews.review_description:
Great mix between an escape game and a narrative game, way more towards the narrative aspect though.
Felt great to play, lovely art style, especially in MR. I do have a great room.

As a Quest 3 user this is one of the most immersive and fun VR/MR games that I have played in a while. You're immediately pulled in as a detective to solve a case... I found that using hand gestures and that extra level of realism to the game. Definitely a must play, one of my top games of summer.
I finished this game in 3 days and really enjoyed it. I would love to see more stories added with alternative endings that would be really cool

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for this amazing comment ☺️ it truly means a lot!
I'm so happy you enjoyed the game and let yourself get carried away by the story.
Alternative endings are a fantastic idea, let me think about it !

You can join our Discord if you'd like to chat more with the team https://discord.gg/yDJjPjuTjb
Thanks again for your support!

reviews.review_description: Detective VR brilliantly combines virtual reality and mixed reality to deliver an outstanding detective experience on the Meta Quest. You’ll find yourself collecting evidence in immersive virtual environments and then conducting investigations right from your own space in mixed reality, seamlessly blending the two formats. Although the gameplay loop can feel repetitive, I never found it dull. There are areas where gameplay and presentation could be enhanced, but the overall experience remains highly engaging, showcasing a fantastic integration of VR and MR. It will be exciting to see if the developers choose to expand on this concept with a sequel or episodic updates that introduce new cases over time. Regardless, Detective VR provides a fun and captivating experience.

The developers are very active and open to any suggestion, keeping the game updated and try their best to get the full experience of the MR and VR headset. Worth the price and the time you will spend. Have fun and best wishes for the devs.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for this review !

reviews.review_description: So much fun playing this game! You quickly get into the shoes of a detective trying to solve a complex case. Being able to handle evidences with your bare hands and to replay the scenes and interviews is very captivating. 5/5!

reviews.review_title: There is a big problem with Korean
reviews.review_description:
If you're playing in Korean, most of the text will be output to □□□□□□ 
The font is broken and unreadable.
The developers need to fix this quickly.
---------- 
added comment: 
Thanks for patch news, Dev!
I was going to post a Korean review on YouTube today, but pushed it back after the patch. 
Thanks for the quick response.

developer_response.body:
I'm glad the patch fixed everything 
So happy it works ! 

If you see some bugs or mistake, please tell me we will fix it soon as possible !
You can write us at contact@detective-vr.com or join our discord here https://discord.gg/yDJjPjuTjb

reviews.review_description:
opinion from a playtester: 
Detective VR is a masterpiece! 
From the moment I stepped into the investigation, I was completely immersed. The storytelling is gripping, with complex characters. The mechanics (examining fingerprints, analyzing blood, presenting evidence)felt like Ace Attorney in real life, but even better. 

The motion-captured performances and stunning visuals make every scene feel real. 
This is not just a game; it’s an experience.  Absolutely brilliant!

I don't think I could have

 more fun than playing this game. It feels like I'm right in the room solving the case. Love the characters an the way the game is played.
Great game with an exciting storyline. The developers are very communicative and solve any problems you have with the game. I highly recommend it.
Well written game. I loved the story and had a lot of fun with the game play. I highly recommend!

developer_response.body: Thank you so much ! We're so glad you enjoyed our game !
